# Can Bankruptcy Eliminate Tax Debt? 5 Rules (2026 Guide)
> Yes, bankruptcy can wipe out some tax debt. Learn the 5 rules that determine if your IRS or state tax debt qualifies for discharge under Chapte...

## About
bankruptcytaxes.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- The 240-Day Rule -- IRS Assessment and Bankruptcy Tax Discharge: https://bankruptcytaxes.org/240-day-rule.html
- The 3-Year Rule for Discharging Tax Debt in Bankruptcy: https://bankruptcytaxes.org/3-year-rule.html
- Filing Tax Returns During Chapter 13 Bankruptcy: https://bankruptcytaxes.org/income-tax-return.html
- Can Bankruptcy Eliminate Tax Debt? 5 Rules (2026 Guide): https://bankruptcytaxes.org/
- IRS Payment Plan vs. Bankruptcy -- Which Is Better for Tax Debt?: https://bankruptcytaxes.org/irs-payment-plan.html
- Payroll Taxes and Bankruptcy -- Trust Fund Taxes Are Never Dischargeable: https://bankruptcytaxes.org/payroll-taxes.html
- Can State Taxes Be Discharged in Bankruptcy?: https://bankruptcytaxes.org/state-taxes.html
- Tax Liens Survive Bankruptcy -- What You Need to Know: https://bankruptcytaxes.org/tax-liens.html
- Can the Bankruptcy Trustee Take My Tax Refund?: https://bankruptcytaxes.org/tax-refund.html
- The Trust Fund Recovery Penalty (100% Penalty) and Bankruptcy: https://bankruptcytaxes.org/trust-fund-penalty.html

## License
Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
